metadata.description: 变量作用域: Python variable resolution follows the LEGB rule: Local → Enclosing → Global → Built-in. To provide a brief Python code example that illustrates this concept: ```python # Define a global variable global_var = "I am global" def outer_function(): # Define an enclosing (non-local) variable enclosing_var = "I am enclosing" def inner_function(): # Define a local variable local_var = "I am local" print(local_var) print(enclosing_var) print(global_var) inner_function() outer_function() ``` In this example: - `local_var` is a local variable within the `inner_function`. - `enclosing_var` is an enclosing (non-local) variable in relation to `inner_function`. - `global_var` is a global variable that can be accessed from any function. When you run this code, it will output: ``` I am local I am enclosing I am global ```
